chunks.text: 50-30-20 Budgeting Rule adapted for India: 50% Needs — rent, groceries, utilities, EMIs, insurance premiums, commute. 30% Wants — dining out, OTT subscriptions, shopping, travel, entertainment. 20% Savings & Investments — SIP, PPF, emergency fund, FD, NPS. For Mumbai/Bengaluru/Delhi where rent is high, adjust to 60-20-20. Key: pay yourself first — auto-transfer savings on salary day.

Emergency Fund: Target: 6 months of monthly expenses (3 months minimum). Where to keep: Liquid Mutual Fund (better than savings account, instant redemption T+1), or high-interest savings account (IDFC, Kotak 7%). Single income household: 9-12 months. If EMIs are high: keep more buffer. Do NOT invest emergency fund in stocks or ELSS. Replenish immediately after use.

Recommended Salary Allocation (India, ₹50,000-₹1,00,000/month): Rent: max 30% of take-home. Groceries: ₹5,000-10,000. Transport: ₹2,000-5,000. Utilities (electricity, phone, internet): ₹2,000-4,000. SIP/investments: minimum 20%. Entertainment/dining: 10-15%. Clothes/shopping: 5%. Emergency fund build-up: 5%. Review budget every 3 months. Track with apps: ET Money, Walnut, Money Manager.

Debt Management in India: Avalanche method — pay highest interest debt first (personal loan 18%, credit card 36%). Snowball — pay smallest balance first for motivation. Never pay only credit card minimum — the interest trap. EMI-to-income ratio should not exceed 40-50%. Debt consolidation loan: single lower-rate loan to clear multiple high-rate debts. CIBIL improvement: on-time payment is #1 factor (35% weightage).

India City Cost of Living (approx monthly 2024): Mumbai 1BHK rent: ₹20,000-50,000. Bengaluru: ₹15,000-35,000. Delhi NCR: ₹12,000-30,000. Pune: ₹12,000-25,000. Hyderabad: ₹12,000-28,000. Groceries family of 4: ₹8,000-15,000. Utilities+internet: ₹3,000-5,000. School fees: ₹5,000-25,000/month. Petrol (full tank): ₹4,000-5,000. Dining out per person: ₹300-800 (mid-range).

Retirement Planning India: Target corpus: 25-30x annual expenses (4% withdrawal rule). At ₹1L/month expenses: need ₹3-3.6 Crore corpus. Inflation adjustment: 6-7% India inflation — double expenses every 10-12 years. Best instruments: NPS (low cost, tax benefit), EPF, ELSS, balanced advantage funds. Start at 25 not 45 — difference is 10x corpus. Rule of 72: at 8% return, money doubles every 9 years. Post-retirement: shift to debt/hybrid for stability.

Financial Goals Framework: Short-term (< 3 years): FD, RD, liquid funds, short-term debt funds. Medium-term (3-5 years): ELSS, balanced hybrid funds, NSC. Long-term (5+ years): equity mutual funds, stocks, PPF, NPS, real estate. SMART goals: Specific (₹10L for car), Measurable (SIP calculator), Achievable (check income), Relevant (priority), Time-bound (3 years). Review and rebalance portfolio every 6 months.

Food Delivery Spending in India: Average Swiggy/Zomato order: ₹350-500. Ordering 3x/week: ₹5,400-6,500/month. Annual: ₹65,000-78,000. Opportunity cost: ₹6,000/month invested in SIP at 12% for 10 years = ₹14 lakhs. Tips to reduce: meal prep Sunday, cook 4 days/week, use Swiggy One/Zomato Gold subscription (₹149-299/month, saves 20-40%). Target food delivery budget: max ₹2,000-3,000/month.

OTT Subscription Costs India 2024: Netflix — ₹149/month (mobile), ₹499 (HD), ₹649 (4K). Amazon Prime — ₹1,499/year (₹125/month). Disney+ Hotstar — ₹149/month (Super), ₹299 (Premium). Sony LIV — ₹299/month. ZEE5 — ₹99/month. Spotify — ₹119/month. YouTube Premium — ₹139/month. All combined: ₹1,500-2,000/month. Tip: share plans with family, rotate subscriptions monthly to cut cost to ₹300-400.
